code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. See SPEC §9.
code.title: Code

old_token_grace_seconds.description: How long the previous token continues to authenticate.
old_token_grace_seconds.title: Old Token Grace Seconds

token.description: The new bearer token in plaintext. Save it now; the API never returns it again. The previous token continues to authenticate for `old_token_grace_seconds` so callers have time to switch.
token.title: Token

name.description: Operator-friendly label for the process. Free-form.
name.title: Name

info.description:
Control plane for an LLM-agent sandbox service. See
[SPECIFICATION.md](https://github.com/JISUlicious/sandboxing/blob/main/SPECIFICATION.md)
and
[ARCHITECTURE.md](https://github.com/JISUlicious/sandboxing/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md)
for the full contract.

Every session is a long-lived, hardened container. Filesystem state in
`/workspace` persists across exec calls and across stop/resume; process
state does not (SPEC-002). All endpoints under `/v1/sessions` require a
bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
info.title: Sandbox Service

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is up. Does not check Docker — see `/readyz`.
Prometheus text exposition (SPEC-503). No auth — bind to an internal port or restrict via reverse proxy in production.

Returns the health of subsystems the API needs to serve mutations: `docker` for the daemon, `audit` for the fail-closed audit log (ARCH §7). All-true means the service will accept new lifecycle / exec / file calls.

post.description: Creates a long-lived sandbox session under the calling tenant. Starts in `RUNNING` with an empty `/workspace` (SPEC-101). `limits` are merged with tenant-default values; per-field caps are enforced (SPEC-100, SPEC-300).

429.description: Tenant concurrency or per-field limit exceeded (`limit_exceeded`). SPEC §6.

delete.description: Removes the container and its `/workspace` volume. Irreversible (SPEC-105). The destroy is multi-step (DESTROYING → DESTROYED) and idempotent on partial failure (ARCH-051).

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).
Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

get.summary: Get a session's status and limits

post.description:
Runs `argv` inside the session and returns stdout/stderr/exit after the process exits or `timeout_s` is hit. Output is capped at 8 MiB per stream independently (SPEC-203); `truncated_streams` lists any that hit the cap. Stdin must be UTF-8 ≤ 1 MiB; binary stdin is a future enhancement.

STOPPED / IDLE sessions are transparently resumed before the exec runs (SPEC-104). `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, and `NO_PROXY` are forbidden in `env` (SPEC-201).

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

408.description: Exec exceeded its wall-clock budget (`exec_timeout`). SPEC-201.

409.description: Session is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (`invalid_state`).

post.description:
Same contract as `/exec` but streams output live as SSE events.

**Event types** (each is an SSE frame with `event:` + `data:`):
- `event: stdout` / `event: stderr` — `{"chunk_b64": "<base64>"}`. Decoded chunks reconstruct the original byte stream in order. Once the per-stream 8 MiB cap is hit, no further chunks of that stream are emitted.
- `event: truncated` — `{"stream": "stdout"|"stderr"}`. Emitted exactly once per affected stream when the cap is reached.
- `event: result` — final ExecResponse-shaped payload (matches the synchronous `/exec` response, SPEC-201/202).
- `event: error` — `{"message": "..."}`. The producer thread crashed; the client should treat this as a 5xx.

Stdin is rejected with 400 — combining stdin with live demuxed streaming is a future slice. OpenAPI tooling does not generate useful clients for SSE; consume with a streaming HTTP client.

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

409.description: Session is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (`invalid_state`).

get.description: Lists immediate children of `dir` (relative to `/workspace`; default is `/workspace` itself).

post.description: Writes a single file into `/workspace`. `content_b64` is base64-encoded for binary safety. Path is validated against traversal (`..`, absolute paths, NUL bytes) per SPEC-107. Parent directories are created as needed. STOPPED / IDLE sessions are transparently resumed (ARCH §3.3).

schema.title: Response Write File V1 Sessions  Session Id  Files Post

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

delete.description: Deletes a file under `/workspace`. Directories require `?recursive=true`; deleting `/workspace` itself is rejected (SPEC-107). Missing paths return 404.

parameters.description: Required to delete a non-empty directory.
parameters.name: recursive

schema.description: Required to delete a non-empty directory.
schema.title: Recursive

get.description: Returns the file's raw bytes as `application/octet-stream`. The unix mode is exposed in the `X-File-Mode` response header. Reading a directory returns 400; missing path returns 404.

Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

post.description: Restarts the container for a `STOPPED` or `IDLE` session. Process state from before the stop is gone — only filesystem state persists (ARCH §3.4).

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

409.description: Session is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (`invalid_state`).

post.description: Stops the underlying container; the volume and `/workspace` contents are retained (SPEC-104). Resume implicitly via `/exec` or any file op, or explicitly via `/resume`.

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

409.description: Session is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (`invalid_state`).

post.description: Issues a new bearer token for the calling tenant and marks the current token revoked-at = now + grace seconds. Both tokens authenticate during the grace window so callers can read the response and switch over without downtime. After the window the old token returns 401. SPEC-405.
post.summary: Rotate the calling tenant's bearer token
